summary: US maintains 4 core requirement(s) under basel iii capital requirements. 2 cross-jurisdiction divergence(s) were identified (2 high, 0 medium, 0 low severity), detailed in 'contradictions'.

key_rules.title: Common Equity Tier 1 Minimum Ratio
key_rules.description: Banking organizations must maintain a minimum Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratio of 4.5% of risk-weighted assets.

key_rules.title: Capital Conservation Buffer
key_rules.description: An additional capital conservation buffer of 2.5% is required on top of minimum risk-based capital ratios.

key_rules.title: Supplementary Leverage Ratio (GSIBs)
key_rules.description: Global systemically important banks must maintain a supplementary leverage ratio of at least 5.0%, inclusive of a 2.0% buffer.

key_rules.title: Liquidity Coverage Ratio
key_rules.description: Covered banking organizations must maintain high-quality liquid assets sufficient to meet net cash outflows over a 30-day stress period, expressed as a minimum LCR of 100%.

contradictions.description: US requires 5.0% for 'Supplementary Leverage Ratio (GSIBs)', while EU requires 3.0% for the equivalent rule ('Leverage Ratio Requirement') — a 40.0% relative divergence.
US requires 5.0% for 'Supplementary Leverage Ratio (GSIBs)', while UK requires 3.25% for the equivalent rule ('UK Leverage Ratio') — a 35.0% relative divergence.
